tagline: Calculate optimal layer height based on nozzle size
keywords: layer height, 3D printing, nozzle size, print quality
calcFn: var maxLayer = nozzle * 0.75; var qualityMult = {'Draft (fast)': 0.75, 'Standard': 0.5, 'High Detail': 0.35, 'Ultra Fine': 0.2}; var mult = qualityMult[quality] || 0.5; var layerHeight = nozzle * mult; return {layerHeight: fmt(layerHeight,2), maxLayer: fmt(maxLayer,2)};

faq.q: What layer height should I use?
faq.a: 25-75% of nozzle diameter. 0.2mm is standard for a 0.4mm nozzle.
Thicker layers bond better but show more visible lines.

formulas.name: Layer Height
formulas.explanation: Fraction of nozzle diameter based on desired quality.

tagline: Calculate the true cost per gram of 3D printing
keywords: 3D print cost, filament cost, cost per gram, printing cost
calcFn: var costPerGram = spoolPrice / spoolWeight; var filamentCost = costPerGram * partWeight; var electricCost = 0.15 * printHours * electricRate; var totalCost = filamentCost + electricCost; return {filamentCost: dollar(filamentCost), electricCost: dollar(electricCost), totalCost: dollar(totalCost)};

faq.a: Filament costs $0.02-0.08/gram depending on material. PLA is cheapest.
Electricity, printer depreciation, failed prints, and post-processing time.

formulas.name: Part Cost
formulas.formula: Cost = (Spool Price / Weight) × Part Weight + Electricity

tagline: Estimate print weight and time based on infill percentage
keywords: infill, 3D print weight, print time, infill percentage
calcFn: var density = 1.24; var solidFraction = 0.3 + (infill / 100) * 0.7; var weight = volume * density * solidFraction; var filamentLength = weight / 3; var printTime = (volume * solidFraction) / 15; return {weight: fmt(weight,1), filamentLength: fmt(filamentLength,1), printTime: fmt(printTime,1)};

faq.q: What infill percentage should I use?
faq.a: 10-20% for decorative, 40-60% for functional, 100% for maximum strength.
Strength increases rapidly up to 40% infill, with diminishing returns after.

formulas.name: Estimated Weight
formulas.explanation: Solid fraction increases with infill percentage.

tagline: Calculate optimal retraction settings to prevent stringing
keywords: retraction, stringing, 3D print settings, bowden, direct drive
calcFn: var distances = {'Direct Drive': {'PLA': 1, 'PETG': 1.5, 'ABS': 1, 'TPU': 0.5}, 'Bowden Tube': {'PLA': 5, 'PETG': 6, 'ABS': 5, 'TPU': 3}}; var speeds = {'Direct Drive': 25, 'Bowden Tube': 40}; var distance = distances[extruder][material] || 2; var speed = speeds[extruder] || 30; var tip = nozzle > 220 ? 'Higher temps need more retraction - add 0.5mm' : 'Temperature is in good range'; return {distance: fmt(distance,1), speed: fmt(speed,0), tip: tip};

faq.a: Melted filament oozing during travel moves. Retraction pulls filament back to stop this.
Direct drive needs 0.5-2mm retraction, bowden needs 4-7mm due to tube play.

formulas.name: Retraction Distance
formulas.formula: Distance based on extruder type and material
formulas.explanation: Direct drive uses shorter retraction than bowden setups.

tagline: Estimate support material usage for overhanging prints
keywords: support material, overhang, 3D print supports, material waste
calcFn: var supportWeight = partWeight * (overhangPct / 100) * (supportDensity / 100) * 2; var totalWeight = partWeight + supportWeight; var wastePct = (supportWeight / totalWeight) * 100; return {supportWeight: fmt(supportWeight,1), totalWeight: fmt(totalWeight,1), wastePct: fmt(wastePct,1)};

faq.a: Overhangs greater than 45° and bridges longer than 10mm typically need support.
Orient the part to reduce overhangs, use tree supports, and lower support density.

formulas.name: Support Weight
formulas.explanation: Proportional to overhang area and support density.

tagline: Get bed adhesion settings for your material and bed type
keywords: bed adhesion, first layer, 3D print bed, warping
calcFn: var temps = {'PLA': 60, 'PETG': 80, 'ABS': 100, 'Nylon': 80, 'TPU': 50}; var bedTemp = temps[material] || 60; var sizeAdj = {'Small (<50mm)': 0, 'Medium (50-150mm)': 0, 'Large (>150mm)': 5}; bedTemp += sizeAdj[partSize] || 0; var adhesionType = material === 'ABS' ? 'Brim + ABS slurry' : material === 'Nylon' ? 'Brim + glue stick' : partSize === 'Large (>150mm)' ? 'Brim recommended' : 'Skirt only'; var firstLayerSpeed = 20; return {bedTemp: fmt(bedTemp,0), adhesionType: adhesionType, firstLayerSpeed: fmt(firstLayerSpeed,0)};

faq.a: Uneven cooling causes corners to curl up. Higher bed temps and enclosures help.
Extra first-layer rings around the part to increase adhesion area.

formulas.name: Bed Temp
formulas.explanation: Material-specific temperature with adjustment for larger parts.

tagline: Calculate buffer solution preparation for target pH
keywords: pH buffer, Henderson-Hasselbalch, buffer solution, chemistry
calcFn: var ratio = Math.pow(10, targetPH - pKa); var totalMoles = totalConc * volume; var baseMoles = totalMoles * ratio / (1 + ratio); var acidMoles = totalMoles - baseMoles; return {ratio: fmt(ratio,2), baseMoles: fmt(baseMoles,2), acidMoles: fmt(acidMoles,2)};

faq.a: pH = pKa + log([A-]/[HA]), relating pH to the ratio of conjugate base to weak acid.
Buffers work best within ±1 pH unit of the pKa value.

formulas.name: Henderson-Hasselbalch
formulas.explanation: Fundamental equation for buffer pH calculation.

tagline: Convert between wavelength, frequency, and energy
keywords: wavelength, frequency, electromagnetic spectrum, photon energy
calcFn: var freqHz = 3e17 / wavelength; var frequency = freqHz / 1e12; var energy = 1240 / wavelength; var spectrum = wavelength < 10 ? 'X-ray' : wavelength < 400 ? 'Ultraviolet' : wavelength < 700 ? 'Visible Light' : wavelength < 1000 ? 'Near Infrared' : 'Infrared'; return {frequency: fmt(frequency,2), energy: fmt(energy,3), spectrum: spectrum};

faq.q: What is the visible light range?
faq.a: 400nm (violet) to 700nm (red) wavelength.

faq.q: How are wavelength and frequency related?
faq.a: Inversely: c = wavelength × frequency. Higher frequency means shorter wavelength.

formulas.name: Frequency
formulas.explanation: Speed of light divided by wavelength.

keywords: ideal gas law, PV=nRT, gas volume, chemistry, physics
calcFn: var R = 0.08206; var tempK = temperature + 273.15; var volume = (moles * R * tempK) / pressure; var volumeM3 = volume / 1000; return {volume: fmt(volume,3), volumeM3: fmt(volumeM3,5)};

faq.q: What is the ideal gas law?
faq.a: PV = nRT relates pressure, volume, moles, and temperature of an ideal gas.
The gas constant: 0.08206 L·atm/(mol·K) or 8.314 J/(mol·K).

formulas.name: Ideal Gas Law
formulas.explanation: Volume from moles, temperature, and pressure.

tagline: Calculate remaining radioactive material after decay
calcFn: var halvings = elapsed / halfLife; var remaining = initial * Math.pow(0.5, halvings); var decayed = initial - remaining; return {remaining: fmt(remaining,4), decayed: fmt(decayed,4), halvings: fmt(halvings,2)};

faq.a: The time for half of a radioactive substance to decay.
5,730 years, used for dating organic materials up to ~50,000 years old.

formulas.name: Radioactive Decay
formulas.explanation: Remaining amount after t time with half-life t½.

tagline: Calculate molarity from mass, volume, and molar mass
calcFn: var moles = mass / molarMass; var volumeL = volume / 1000; var molarity = moles / volumeL; var normality = molarity; return {moles: fmt(moles,4), molarity: fmt(molarity,4), normality: fmt(normality,4)};

faq.a: Moles of solute per liter of solution (M = mol/L).

faq.q: How do I find molar mass?
faq.a: Sum the atomic masses of all atoms in the molecular formula. NaCl = 22.99 + 35.45 = 58.44.

formulas.name: Molarity
formulas.formula: M = (mass / molar mass) / volume in L
formulas.explanation: Moles divided by volume in liters.

tagline: Calculate gravitational attraction between two masses
keywords: gravity, gravitational force, Newton's law, F=Gm1m2/r2
calcFn: var G = 6.674e-11; var force = G * mass1 * mass2 / (distance * distance); var accel = force / mass2; return {force: fmt(force,4), accel: fmt(accel,4)};

faq.a: The gravitational constant: 6.674 × 10⁻¹¹ N·m²/kg².

faq.q: Why does distance matter so much?
faq.a: Force decreases with the square of distance — doubling distance reduces force to 1/4.

formulas.name: Newton's Law of Gravitation
formulas.explanation: Force between two masses separated by distance r.

tagline: Calculate resin and hardener amounts for your mold
keywords: resin, epoxy resin, resin art, mold volume, resin mixing
calcFn: var cubicIn = length * width * depth; var totalOz = cubicIn * 0.554; var ratios = {'1:1': [0.5,0.5], '2:1': [0.667,0.333], '3:1': [0.75,0.25]}; var r = ratios[mixRatio] || [0.5,0.5]; var resinOz = totalOz * r[0] * 1.1; var hardenerOz = totalOz * r[1] * 1.1; return {totalOz: fmt(totalOz,1), resinOz: fmt(resinOz,1), hardenerOz: fmt(hardenerOz,1)};

faq.q: How much resin do I need?
faq.a: Calculate mold volume in cubic inches, multiply by 0.554 to get fluid ounces.
Yes, mix 10% extra to account for mixing cup residue and bubbles.

formulas.name: Volume
formulas.explanation: Cubic inches to fluid ounces conversion.

tagline: Estimate yarn needed for knitting or crochet projects
keywords: yarn, yardage, knitting, crochet, yarn estimate
calcFn: var yards = {'Scarf': {'Lace': 600,'Fingering': 500,'Sport': 400,'Worsted': 300,'Bulky': 200,'Super Bulky': 150}, 'Hat': {'Lace': 400,'Fingering': 300,'Sport': 250,'Worsted': 200,'Bulky': 150,'Super Bulky': 100}, 'Sweater (Adult)': {'Lace': 3000,'Fingering': 2500,'Sport': 2000,'Worsted': 1500,'Bulky': 1200,'Super Bulky': 900}, 'Blanket (Throw)': {'Lace': 4000,'Fingering': 3500,'Sport': 2800,'Worsted': 2200,'Bulky': 1800,'Super Bulky': 1400}, 'Socks (pair)': {'Lace': 500,'Fingering': 400,'Sport': 350,'Worsted': 300,'Bulky': 250,'Super Bulky': 200}, 'Baby Blanket': {'Lace': 1800,'Fingering': 1500,'Sport': 1200,'Worsted': 1000,'Bulky': 800,'Super Bulky': 600}}; var yardage = yards[projectType][yarnWeight] || 500; var skeins = Math.ceil(yardage / 200); var cost = skeins * 8; return {yardage: fmt(yardage,0), skeins: fmt(skeins,0), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: How much yarn for a blanket?
faq.a: A throw blanket needs 1400-4000 yards depending on yarn weight.
Always buy 10-15% extra to account for gauge differences and mistakes.

formulas.name: Skeins
formulas.explanation: Total yardage divided by what each skein provides.

tagline: Calculate wax weight and fragrance oil for candle making
keywords: candle making, wax weight, fragrance oil, candle supplies
calcFn: var waxOzPer = containerOz * 0.86; var totalWaxOz = waxOzPer * containers; var waxLbs = totalWaxOz / 16; var fragOz = (totalWaxOz * fragPct / 100); var wicks = containers; return {waxLbs: fmt(waxLbs,1), fragOz: fmt(fragOz,1), wicks: fmt(wicks,0)};

faq.a: Multiply container fluid ounces by 0.86 to get wax weight in ounces.

faq.q: What is the max fragrance load?
faq.a: Most waxes handle 6-10%. Exceeding the limit causes sweating and poor burns.

formulas.name: Wax Weight
formulas.explanation: Wax is lighter than water, so use 86% of container volume.

tagline: Estimate usable leather from a hide
keywords: leather, hide, leather craft, square feet, leather yield
calcFn: var usable = hideSize * (1 - waste/100); var pieceSqFt = pieceArea / 144; var pieces = Math.floor(usable / pieceSqFt); return {usable: fmt(usable,1), pieces: fmt(pieces,0)};

faq.q: How much waste from a leather hide?
faq.a: Typically 20-30% waste from edges, belly, and defects.

faq.q: What size hide do I need?
faq.a: A full cowhide is 40-55 sq ft, a half hide is 20-25 sq ft.

formulas.name: Usable Area
formulas.explanation: Total hide minus defects and unusable edges.

tagline: Calculate thread needed for your embroidery project
keywords: embroidery, thread, floss, cross stitch, needlework
calcFn: var inchesPerStitch = {'14 count Aida': 0.14, '16 count Aida': 0.125, '18 count Aida': 0.11, '28 count linen': 0.07}; var ips = inchesPerStitch[fabric] || 0.14; var totalInches = stitches * ips * strands * 1.3; var lengthYards = totalInches / 36; var skeins = Math.ceil(lengthYards / 8.7); return {lengthYards: fmt(lengthYards,1), skeins: fmt(skeins,0)};

faq.q: How much thread per cross stitch?
faq.a: About 1 inch of thread per stitch on 14-count Aida with 2 strands.
Add 20-30% extra for tails, starting, and ending threads.

formulas.name: Thread Length
formulas.explanation: Total stitches times per-stitch thread use with 30% waste factor.

tagline: Calculate glaze mixing ratios by weight
keywords: pottery, glaze, ceramic, glaze recipe, mixing
calcFn: var perPot = 150; var totalNeeded = pots * perPot; var dryWeight = Math.max(batchSize, totalNeeded * 0.5); var waterWeight = dryWeight * (waterRatio / 100); var totalVolume = dryWeight * 0.4 + waterWeight; return {dryWeight: fmt(dryWeight,0), waterWeight: fmt(waterWeight,0), totalVolume: fmt(totalVolume,0)};

faq.a: About 100-200mL per medium pot, depending on application thickness.

faq.q: What is the ideal water ratio?
faq.a: Most glazes use 80-120% water by weight of dry materials for dipping consistency.

formulas.name: Water Amount
formulas.explanation: Water added as a percentage of total dry ingredients.
